news.t: Arm deal is expected to lift tech IPOs, but not all future debuts will have the same draw
How to use ETFs to play Ant Group's historic Shanghai-Hong Kong dual listing, possibly one of the biggest IPOs ever

holdings.infoBox: IPOS is an equity ETF with a total of 50 individual holdings. The top holdings are Kioxia Holdings stock at 24.35%, Contemporary Amperex Techn H Common Stock Cny1.0 at 9.35%, Galderma Group AG Registered Shares at 9.14%, JX Advanced Metals at 6.14%, and Kokusai Electric at 5.03%.

overview.description: The Renaissance International IPO ETF (IPOS) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Renaissance International IPO index. The fund tracks a cap-weighted index of recent IPOs listed internationally. The fund adds an IPO within three years of its initial listing. IPOS was launched on Oct 6, 2014 and is issued by Renaissance.

data.title: Arm deal is expected to lift tech IPOs, but not all future debuts will have the same draw
data.text: The strong debut of Arm Holdings Plc. is expected to reignite the long-dormant IPO market for tech companies, but not all the current deals in the pipeline will have the same draw for investors.

data.title: Many 2021 IPOs fall flat on first trading day. Unpacking the performance
data.text: Renaissance Capital Chairman, principal and co-founder Kathleen Smith breaks down why some of this year's initial public offerings haven't performed as well as expected. With CNBC's Bob Pisani.

data.title: Ant Group IPO: These ETFs could be first to have access, analyst says
data.text: Todd Rosenbluth, senior director of ETF and mutual fund research at CFRA Research, flags the ETFs to watch in relation to the Ant Group IPO, which could be the largest debut in history.

data.title: How to use ETFs to play Ant Group's historic Shanghai-Hong Kong dual listing, possibly one of the biggest IPOs ever
data.text: ETF analysts share some ways U.S. investors can get in on Alipay parent Ant Group's upcoming dual listing in Shanghai and Hong Kong.

data.title: How to play Ant Group's dual listing, possibly one of the biggest IPOs ever
